post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 213 (Newbie Game 85): Avengers Mafia

If you're not currently alive in this game, do not post. If you want to join a game, go to the Signups thread. If you want to learn how Mafia works, see the FAQ thread. (The game is over.)

Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas among others for the rule template.
  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications or anything else.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any communication from the mods, including role PMs. Paraphrase!
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere (except in places allowed by the mod). You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not abuse loopholes.

  • Voting and Hanging
    • This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the greatest number of votes at the deadline will be hanged.
    • If there is a tie at the deadline, the hang will be randomly chosen from the players with the greatest number of votes.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Spell the name correctly, please.
    • Votes in quotes or hide or tip tags will not be counted.
    • Votes with typos ("Voet: fdas" for example) may be counted as long as the intent is clear, though I strongly prefer that you follow the above formatting. Accidental votes that are clearly not intentional will not count. If you vote incorrectly there's no guarantee that it will or will not be counted; I'm just trying to be a bit more generous with typos and such. (Don't try to abuse loopholes. This isn't a game of "be annoying as possible")
    • Despite the above rule, still be careful about hammering if you are mafia. If you're unsure whether something is a real vote, wait until it's confirmed in a votecount.
    • Votes are locked in LyLo.
    • You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority, but only for no hang.
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • The moment the deadline passes (or majority is reached, if it is enabled), no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Play the game. The moderators will prod you first and then replace you (36 hours after a prod) if you don't play the game. Generally, you will be prodded if you do not post for 36 hours.
    • If the moderators get tired of repeatedly prodding you, you'll be replaced.
    • Each day will be 12 days (288 hours). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 288 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 2 days after night begins. If all living players agree, the night phase will end early.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed.
    • If a player is going to be in V/LA (vacation/limited access; i.e., less available to play the game for some time), they must post in bold in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player during the V/LA unless the V/LA is too long.
    • Mafia may access their QT during the day, and are encouraged to use it. Same with Masons.

  • Etiquette
    •  Do not use inappropriate language.
    •  Do not make any personal attacks.
    •  Follow all of the rules.
    •  Be mature. Don't spam. If you've read the rules say so in your role PM.
    •  For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.

  • Moderators
    • chess24 is the moderator of Mafia Game 213 and bigmath is the co-moderator.
    • Don't post in color. If you do have to post in color, try to avoid green, blue, and red. (using these colors is fine as long as your post doesn't resemble a mod post)
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules.
  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the NewD3 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. One row and column are selected at random to provide 2 town and 1 scum roles. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The 9 possible setups are:

A1: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker vs. Town Cop, Town Doctor, 5x Town Vanilla
A2: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker vs. Town Jailkeeper, Town Tracker, 5x Town Vanilla
A3: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker vs. Town Mason, Town Mason, 5x Town Vanilla
B1: Mafia Goon, Mafia Rolecop vs. Town Tracker, Town Friendly Neighbor, 5x Town Vanilla
B2: Mafia Goon, Mafia Rolecop vs. Town Jailkeeper, Town Friendly Neighbor, 5x Town Vanilla
B3: Mafia Goon, Mafia Rolecop vs. Town Tracker, Town Doctor, 5x Town Vanilla
C1: Mafia Goon, Mafia Goon vs. Town Cop, 6x Town Vanilla
C2: Mafia Goon, Mafia Goon vs. Town Jailkeeper, 6x Town Vanilla
C3: Mafia Goon, Mafia Goon vs. Town Mason, Town Mason, 5x Town Vanilla

If, in setup A2, the Mafia Roleblocker and the Town Jailkeeper target each other, the Mafia Roleblocker takes precedence.

Important rules for experienced players who are already familiar with the rules (though you should read them anyway, to find the confirm rule):
Mafia have daychat. There is deadline hang. Day/night phases are 12 days/2 days.


Role PMs
Town Role PMs
VT wrote:
Hi [player],

You are a Citizen of New York (Vanilla Townie). After the Battle of New York, you want any threats to your city gone and therefore are on the Avengers side (Town). You have no special abilities besides your voice and vote.

You win when all the Mafia are eliminated. You can find the thread HERE.
Jailkeeper wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Captain America (Town Jailkeeper). Using your vibranium shield, each night, you may target a player. They will be protected from kills but, because they have to stay protected behind the shield they will also be prevented from using any actions during that night.

You win when all the Mafia are eliminated. You can find the thread HERE.
Cop wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Black Widow (Town Cop). You are slightly weaker than the rest of the Avengers when it comes to fighting, but when it comes to stealth and hacking, you know what to do. Each night, you may target a player and learn whether they are Town or Mafia.

You win when all the Mafia are eliminated. You can find the thread HERE.
Mason wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Scarlet Witch (Town Mason). You know that your brother, Quicksilver (PLAYER) is Town (and they also know that you are Town). The two of you may communicate at any time via QuickTopic HERE. You two are encouraged to use this to share thoughts with each other!

You win when all the Mafia are eliminated. You can find the thread HERE.
If a mason exists, the other mason will have the above PM but with the names swapped.
Tracker wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Ant-Man (Town Tracker). Due to your incredibly small size, each night, you can transform and target a player to learn who, if anyone, they targeted. (If you are roleblocked, you will receive no result.)

You win when all the Mafia are eliminated. You can find the thread HERE.
Doctor wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Hulk (Town Doctor). Each night, you may target a player. Because of your strength and size, you are able to protect them from kills for that night.

You win when all the Mafia are eliminated. You can find the thread HERE.
Friendly Neighbor wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Nebula (Town Friendly Neighbor). After a past version of yourself betrayed the Avengers, you must convince everyone of your true alignment. Each night, you may target a player. They will be informed that you are Town.

You win when all the Mafia are eliminated. You can find the thread HERE.

Mafia Role PMs
Mafia Goon wrote:
Hi [player],

You are a Child of Thanos (Mafia Goon). Your partner is NAME, Mafia ROLENAME.

Each night, the two of you may kill a player. You must specify one of you to submit the kill. Also, you two may communicate at any time via QuickTopic HERE. You are encouraged to use this to discuss strategy with your partner and plan night actions.

You win when the Town has been eliminated, or nothing can stop this from happening.
Mafia Goon wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Ultron (Mafia Rolecop). Your partner is NAME, Child of Thanos (Mafia Goon).

Each night, the two of you may kill a player. You must specify one of you to submit the kill. Also, you two may communicate at any time via QuickTopic HERE. You are encouraged to use this to discuss strategy with your partner and plan night actions.

Additionally, because of your robot-hacking skills, each night you may target a player and learn their role.

You win when the Town has been eliminated, or nothing can stop this from happening.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
Hi [player],

You are Thanos (Mafia Roleblocker). Your partner is NAME, Child of Thanos (Mafia Goon).

Each night, the two of you may kill a player. You must specify one of you to submit the kill. Also, you two may communicate at any time via QuickTopic HERE. You are encouraged to use this to discuss strategy with your partner and plan night actions.

Additionally, each night you may target a player. Using the Mind Stone to control them, you will prevent them from using any actions.

You win when the Town has been eliminated, or nothing can stop this from happening.


Alive (4)
+HannahW
mathicorn snoozingnewt
mayakalai XrCyclone
Scipio1

Dead (5)
lordoftherings4926 smartatmath b137740, Citizen of New York (Vanilla Townie), hanged Day 1
+aroodman, Captain America (Town Jailkeeper), died Night 1
pog kevinmathz, Citizen of New York (Vanilla Townie), hanged Day 2
BD117 bjc, Ant-Man (Town Tracker), died Night 2
+mathjay, Citizen of New York (Vanilla Townie), hanged Day 3


Significant Events
D1: smartatmath replaces lordoftherings4926
D1: bjc replaces BD117
D1: b137740 replaces smartatmath
D1: XrCyclone replaces mayakalai
D1: kevinmathz replaces pog
Day 1 Hang: b137740 was Vanilla Townie
Night 1 Results: aroodman was Town Jailkeeper
D2: snoozingnewt replaces mathicorn
Day 2 Hang: kevinmathz was Vanilla Townie
Night 2 Results: bjc was Town Tracker
Day 3 Hang: mathjay was Vanilla Townie. Mafia win
Postgame info


Warnings
bjc receives a warning for OOT.
XrCyclone receives a warning for angleshooting, specifically for discussing replacements.


The game is over.

post_rendered:
hihihi
welcome everyoneee im mathjay you can call me jay or mj and im one of the SEs in this game
along with hannahw and aroodman we will do our best to educate you for the big bad world of mafia out there
longer post coming soon^tm with a couple dos and donts
but for now
lets start with a few questions
we call this rqs (random question stage) just to get to know each other a big better
you can add questions if you want!

1. what's your preferred nickname?

post_rendered:
Preliminary things:
  • Read all of the rules and confirm.
  • Answer the RQS.
  • Ask questions if you are confused.
Things to know/do after that:
  • Your first and foremost duty as town is to find scum.
  • I guess my first advice, though is to be active and post -- but that doesn't mean one should just post a bunch of fluff (this word means what it sounds like) that does absolutely nothing and clogs up the thread. If you're ever confused or don't know what's happening, ask questions. Ask questions. Ask people why they think this or that. How they reached a conclusion. What evidence do they have. Why don't they interpret a post this way instead of that way. etc. And do something with those answers. Process them and consider their merit and how much of it you'll believe/disbelieve/put in your back pocket.
  • Note that scum's goal is to not be hanged/suspected. Think about this, draw inferences from it, and keep it in mind as the game proceeds. (Ask me if you have no idea what I'm saying here.)
  • The way I usually find scum is by noting significant posts that might be AI (alignment indicative -- that tell you whether a person is scum or town). I insert my thoughts on it and move on. If I'm not sure, I ask questions/just leave it there for future reference.
  • Things that scum tend to do: bandwagon (follow the crowd), lurk (not show up), pocketing (sweet talking a townie into locking them town), hedging (having no definitive stance/fencesitting), bussing (attacking their partner to seem not w/w, usually in the form of noncommittal attacks), buddying (opposite of bussing for the purpose of making sure their partner is tr'ed, I think).
  • Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.
Terms to know: w/w (wolf/wolf. In other words, two people are scum together.); v/v (villager/villager. Two people are town together.); w/v (self explanatory); AI (alignment indicative); NAI (not alignment indicative); reads (thoughts on who is scum or town); RQS (random question[ing?] stage: What mathjay posted in his first post to get to know the players.); RVS (random voting stage: When people randomly vote to create something to discuss.); meta (how one has played in previous mafia games); wifom (wine in front of me).

mathjay generally has an extensive post, so ig he can fill in whatever I missed. But these are the basics, I think.

post_rendered:
Clarification on something Hannah said:
HannahW wrote:
Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.

More specifically, what effect does this post/action have that town/scum would want? If it's an effect that town would want and not an effect that scum would want, the person in question is likely town. If it's an effect that scum would want that town wouldn't, the player in question is likely scum.

Note that being seen as town is an effect that scum would want, so often a post/action that seems to help town can't necessarily be completely townread, since scum might post something like it to appear townread. However, for obvious reasons, it can't be scumread either.

Since RVS doesn't seem to be much of a thing right now, I kinda want to see what happens if people start trying to make reads immediately. So, everyone try to give a reason why you think someone in this game is town/scum. It can be a terrible reason; you don't need to be confident in your read. Just throw something out. Also try not to just repeat what others say for now.

I'll start. Light TR (townread) on Scipio by tone and the RT attempt.

More serious discussion will happen when we begin to talk about these reads, and then talk about talking about these reads.

post_rendered: Without getting into too much detail, players will only be killed by either the town or the mafia. If a player is killed by the mafia, that player is guaranteed to be town. If a player is killed by the town, that player is not guaranteed to be town. Since town only wins when the mafia are dead, town must maximize the number of town-controlled kills, i. e. hangs. This is why not hanging D1 is bad.

post_rendered:
One last clarification on the No Hang thing:

There is a kind of situation, referred to as MyLo, where not hanging is actually the right thing to do. This is when there are two more townies than mafia, so if you hang a townie, mafia kills another townie that night, and once mafia has half of the vote, they can completely prevent town from hanging them. It's better to pass the day, let mafia kill one townie, and then have a higher chance of hanging mafia the next day, since there are fewer townies to hang incorrectly. However, we're at least a month away from that, if it happens this game at all, so don't worry about it for now.

Does someone have the link for fdas's ultimate mafia guide?

post_rendered:
HannahW wrote:
Preliminary things:
  • Read all of the rules and confirm.
  • Answer the RQS.
  • Ask questions if you are confused.
Things to know/do after that:
  • Your first and foremost duty as town is to find scum.
  • I guess my first advice, though is to be active and post -- but that doesn't mean one should just post a bunch of fluff (this word means what it sounds like) that does absolutely nothing and clogs up the thread. If you're ever confused or don't know what's happening, ask questions. Ask questions. Ask people why they think this or that. How they reached a conclusion. What evidence do they have. Why don't they interpret a post this way instead of that way. etc. And do something with those answers. Process them and consider their merit and how much of it you'll believe/disbelieve/put in your back pocket.
  • Note that scum's goal is to not be hanged/suspected. Think about this, draw inferences from it, and keep it in mind as the game proceeds. (Ask me if you have no idea what I'm saying here.)
  • The way I usually find scum is by noting significant posts that might be AI (alignment indicative -- that tell you whether a person is scum or town). I insert my thoughts on it and move on. If I'm not sure, I ask questions/just leave it there for future reference.
  • Things that scum tend to do: bandwagon (follow the crowd), lurk (not show up), pocketing (sweet talking a townie into locking them town), hedging (having no definitive stance/fencesitting), bussing (attacking their partner to seem not w/w, usually in the form of noncommittal attacks), buddying (opposite of bussing for the purpose of making sure their partner is tr'ed, I think).
  • Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.
Terms to know: w/w (wolf/wolf. In other words, two people are scum together.); v/v (villager/villager. Two people are town together.); w/v (self explanatory); AI (alignment indicative); NAI (not alignment indicative); reads (thoughts on who is scum or town); RQS (random question[ing?] stage: What mathjay posted in his first post to get to know the players.); RVS (random voting stage: When people randomly vote to create something to discuss.); meta (how one has played in previous mafia games); wifom (wine in front of me).

mathjay generally has an extensive post, so ig he can fill in whatever I missed. But these are the basics, I think.

  • Read all of the rules and confirm.
  • Answer the RQS.
  • Ask questions if you are confused.
Things to know/do after that:
  • Your first and foremost duty as town is to find scum.
  • I guess my first advice, though is to be active and post -- but that doesn't mean one should just post a bunch of fluff (this word means what it sounds like) that does absolutely nothing and clogs up the thread. If you're ever confused or don't know what's happening, ask questions. Ask questions. Ask people why they think this or that. How they reached a conclusion. What evidence do they have. Why don't they interpret a post this way instead of that way. etc. And do something with those answers. Process them and consider their merit and how much of it you'll believe/disbelieve/put in your back pocket.
  • Note that scum's goal is to not be hanged/suspected. Think about this, draw inferences from it, and keep it in mind as the game proceeds. (Ask me if you have no idea what I'm saying here.)
  • The way I usually find scum is by noting significant posts that might be AI (alignment indicative -- that tell you whether a person is scum or town). I insert my thoughts on it and move on. If I'm not sure, I ask questions/just leave it there for future reference.
  • Things that scum tend to do: bandwagon (follow the crowd), lurk (not show up), pocketing (sweet talking a townie into locking them town), hedging (having no definitive stance/fencesitting), bussing (attacking their partner to seem not w/w, usually in the form of noncommittal attacks), buddying (opposite of bussing for the purpose of making sure their partner is tr'ed, I think).
  • Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.
Terms to know: w/w (wolf/wolf. In other words, two people are scum together.); v/v (villager/villager. Two people are town together.); w/v (self explanatory); AI (alignment indicative); NAI (not alignment indicative); reads (thoughts on who is scum or town); RQS (random question[ing?] stage: What mathjay posted in his first post to get to know the players.); RVS (random voting stage: When people randomly vote to create something to discuss.); meta (how one has played in previous mafia games); wifom (wine in front of me).

mathjay generally has an extensive post, so ig he can fill in whatever I missed. But these are the basics, I think.

aroodman wrote:
Without getting into too much detail, players will only be killed by either the town or the mafia. If a player is killed by the mafia, that player is guaranteed to be town. If a player is killed by the town, that player is not guaranteed to be town. Since town only wins when the mafia are dead, town must maximize the number of town-controlled kills, i. e. hangs. This is why not hanging D1 is bad.
aroodman wrote:
One last clarification on the No Hang thing:

There is a kind of situation, referred to as MyLo, where not hanging is actually the right thing to do. This is when there are two more townies than mafia, so if you hang a townie, mafia kills another townie that night, and once mafia has half of the vote, they can completely prevent town from hanging them. It's better to pass the day, let mafia kill one townie, and then have a higher chance of hanging mafia the next day, since there are fewer townies to hang incorrectly. However, we're at least a month away from that, if it happens this game at all, so don't worry about it for now.

My thoughts: I disagree with #407 because it neglects some pertinent facts.

Everyone sat around a table, thinking their own thoughts and eyeing one another, some with nervousness, others with suspicion. Then, bjc spoke up

so a small note on status quo
if you rly want to put effort into reading
something a theoretical someone should look at is status quo
and think about the gamestate as of yesterday's hang
all of you know this but scum have two options
they can buddy in order to force a mishang
or they can hard buss to win at f3
if you asked me about whether scum would rather buddy or buss, i would say theyd rather buddy

because the risk is kinda really scary

so if you think scum would buddy
you can look for a mishang theyre pushing
conversely if you think a mishang is being pushed
you can look the people who are pushing it the hardest
and likewise
if you think scum would buss
you can look for who is being hard pushed and minimizing spew and etc
and conversely if you think scum is actually being hard pushed
you can look for who is hard pushing hard enough that when that person flips, the person who hard pushed would be 90% conftown
because otherwise bussing is simply not worth it as a strategy

if you work out the webs of this

some website wrote:
Circumstantial Ad Hominem occurs when someone attacks a claim by saying that the person making the claim is only making it because it's in his/her interest or because of his/her circumstances.
"youre only voting me because there's no one else in poe and im town so fmpov youre scum"
If this is a response to someone accusing someone else of being scum, the sure. It will be a circumstantial ad hominem. It is a fallacious response to an attack. Note that circumstantial ad hominem is not an attack in the sense that it's a stand-alone argument. It is a response to an argument. The attacking parts of that sentence are thus not invalidated by the circumstantial ad hominem. If one would discuss the attacking parts, that would eb a completely separate issue.
"the only reason scum scipio wouldve defended flailing town wouldve been tmi towncred"
This is not circumstantial ad hominem because it is an attack and (as far as I can tell from the implications within the sentence) is not aimed at invalidating another claim. You've also departed not only from the purpose but perhaps from the substance of a circumstantial ad hominem as well: "the person making the claim is only making it because it's in his/her interest or because of his/her circumstances." It is noteworthy that no statement on its own is circumstantial ad hominem since it is a fallacious response, so context matters a lot. Nonetheless, it appears that you have misconstrued my reply to scipio.
What I have said above applies to this last example:
"youre only doing x/y/z because it benefits you/is better for wincon/is scum wincon/etc"
Again, circumstantial ad hominem is when such a phrase is used to refute and attack or claim.

i - Snoozing said that generally if something is too obvious it's probably not true.
ii - Snoozing basically said that he/hw can't be scum because it's too obvious.

snoozing: "if something is too obvious it's probably false"

I) [hide=The premise to premise issue]Premises i and ii are fine, but premise iii cannot be drawn from them: namely the part "to justify his actions." Premises i and ii present an excellent case for snoozing using twtbaw, but they in no way touch upon the purpose to which he is using it. You can't construe that he is using it to justify himself based on those premises, so one must look at context, which is addressed in the next point.[/hide]
II) [hide=The context issue]The context of snoozing posting that is Xr declaring his uncertainty about snoozing/hw, namely because it is so obvious. Snoozing replies in order to suggest to him that snoozing/hw is not the right scum team.
Sure. snoozing's statement would be extremely problematic if it were in the context of defending himself or if it were a serious reason why snoozing/hw is not possible. But it's not. In this context, it's merely a push to guide Xr's thoughts in the right direction since he has been largely ignoring the concrete and substantiated reasons for hw/snoozing not w/w and also the obvious town motivation for hw and snoozing 'buddying.'[/hide]
III) [hide=The confbias issue]The word 'justify' itself is slanted in the direction of snoozing being scum, since it implies that there is something to justify in the first place. Partnership in thread in no way necessitates the two people being scum. It can just as easily be two people being town and mutually townreading each other.
At any rate, my main point here is that even if what Scipio said holds up, it cannot stand as an attack since it requires assuming that snoozing is scum to be an attack.
Let's assume that snoozing primarily said that to clear himself. What does that mean? Nothing.
That plus that the snoozing/hw not w/w case already having been presented (and ignored) makes this 'twtbaw' attack invalid.
